title: ‘Scientific community in PGI clearly knows ways to advance medical knowledge and improve patient care’: AIIMS Dean
description: Talking of Artificial Intelligence in the field of health, the AIIMS Dean noted that AI should not be dealt with in isolation, and should complement health services, for instance, in screening, it can ease the burden of health care workers and improve objectivity and needs regulation.

title: ‘About principle, not people’, OpenAI's robotics head quits after company's Pentagon deal - Who is Caitlin Kalinowski?
description: The head of OpenAI’s robotics team resigned Saturday, citing the company’s deal to deploy its artificial intelligence models within the Pentagon’s classified network as the cause.
content: Caitlin Kalinowski, head of ChatGPT maker OpenAI's robotics team has resigned from the position. In a post on social media platform X (formerly Twitter), she said the decision was directly tied to the company's new deal with the Department of War (Do... [3215 chars]

title: Kerala revises minimum wages for private hospital nurses; UNA to continue stir
description: UNA declared that nurses would not withdraw from the indefinite strike unless the government raises their monthly pay to 40,000..Kerala nurse minimum wage, private hospital nurse salary hike, nursing staff pay revision Kerala, new minimum wage nurses Kerala, AI driven wage analysis nursing, LLM impact on healthcare wages, Kerala nursing wage dispute, healthcare worker salary increase, nurses strike Kerala update, revised nursing pay scale Kerala, healthcare employment laws Kerala, artificial intelligence in wage setting, large language models for salary prediction, Kerala government labour department notification, private healthcare sector wages
content:
Thiruvananthapuram: The Kerala government has revised the minimum wages of nurses working in private hospitals, raising the base pay from ₹25,450 to ₹30,880. The wage structure will vary according to the number of beds in the hospitals.

title: Digital transformation of food retail is reshaping food access for consumers
description: A research report published in the Journal of Nutrition Education and Behavior examines how the rapid digitalization of the retail food environment is reshaping food access in the United States and highlights implications for public health nutrition research, practice, and policy. The authors describe how online grocery platforms, mobile food delivery applications, artificial intelligence (AI), and digital marketing are transforming the way consumers encounter and purchase food.

title: Why AI's beginning doesn't mean the end of your career
description: Thousands of Australian jobs are on the chopping block as companies lean into artificial intelligence, forcing some workers to pivot or be left behind.
content:
Natalie MacDonald was six weeks back from maternity leave when a group email landed at 1am saying she'd been made redundant.
